action.reasoning: Observation: The current pose-aware renderer has been partially edited and likely still has ray-convention/API issues, while the best valid score remains the fixed-orbit baseline LPIPS 0.619. Analysis: The largest likely improvement is still target-camera rendering; to avoid another zero-intersection failure, I will rewrite predict.py cleanly using TripoSR's get_ray_directions/get_rays with focal in pixels, full c2w matrices, safe fallback to default rendering, and a checkpoint-controlled render_mode. I will default to relative target cameras because TripoSR receives only the input image and should render target poses relative to view 0. Decision: Create a relative-mode checkpoint, smoke-test one validation object, then submit evaluation.
action.command: python -c "import torch, os; os.makedirs('checkpoints', exist_ok=True); torch.save({'render_mode':'relative'}, 'checkpoints/relative.pt')" && OBJ=$(python -c "from pathlib import Path; print(sorted(str(p) for p in Path('/data/val').iterdir() if (p/'input.png').exists())[0])") && python predict.py --data_path "$OBJ" --checkpoint checkpoints/relative.pt --output /tmp/relative_smoke.json
